refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation period clarifies the requirement for design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability metrics to system stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Use link health monitoring and predictive failure detection algorithms.
DP3: Provide real-time link status telemetry and alerting.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with failover logic
Telemetry interface dependency on link status
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically supports the FR with manageable coupling between monitoring and failover functions.

assumptions_risks: Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent refinement levels to enhance robustness.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical network hardware and embedded failover control logic within the vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends link status and availability metrics to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections managed for redundancy.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but less direct control over physical link redundancy.
Primary containment of network hardware and failover logic; minimal diffusion.
Receives link status but does not control redundancy mechanisms.
Monitors safety alerts but not involved in link availability control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Placing the DP in the Vehicle module centralizes control of physical network redundancy and failover, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in vehicle embedded systems.
Dependency on vehicle hardware lifecycle and updates.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate integration with Teleoperation module for enhanced monitoring and alerting in next refinement.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions and meets the availability target.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion as failover logic is contained within Vehicle module.
Telemetry interface diffusion is acceptable for status reporting.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor Vehicle module, supporting clear ownership and manageable interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring link availability metrics via Telemetry interface and physical inspection of Vehicle network hardware.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of physical network redundancy to Vehicle module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Plan next level refinement to incorporate link monitoring and alerting functions in Teleoperation module.
Assess safety impact and coordination with Safety Services module.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement is needed to fully address monitoring and alerting aspects and to reduce risks related to safety criticality.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability metrics to system stakeholders.

revise.why: To decompose the high-level FR into atomic sub-FRs that cover monitoring, recovery, and reporting explicitly.

assumptions: Vehicle embedded systems can host failover logic.
evidence_needed: Test data on link availability with redundancy implemented.
